title: Ixora nilagirica
taxonomy: Ixora nilagirica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The genus Ixora is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Ixora nilagirica is specifically found in the Western Ghats mountain range in India. The plant's classification is further detailed at the species level, but its placement within the broader botanical hierarchy is well established.
uses: Ixora nilagirica has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where its leaves and flowers are used to treat various ailments. Ixora nilagirica is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The plant is also used to treat fever and other respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Its leaves are also used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and dysentery. Additionally, Ixora nilagirica is used in traditional medicine to treat menstrual disorders and other gynecological issues.
